properties.remark: MUD AND ROCKS AND OTHER DEBRIS COVERED MOST ROADS IN FORT IRWIN.
A SPOTTER REPORTED A DRIVEWAY WAS WASHED OUT AND 4 TO 6 INCHES OF WATER WAS FLOWING DOWN SOME STREETS IN KINGMAN.
WATER WAS ON LOW SPOTS ON ROADS AND SMALL ROCKS AND BRANCHES WERE ON ROADS IN THE JOSHUA TREE AREA. THIS SPOTTER REPORTED 2.68 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE LAST 24 HOURS.
MUD WAS COVERING ROADS AT FORT IRWIN NORTH OF BARSTOW AND ROADS WERE BEING BARRICATED OFF.
AN ELDERLY CARE CENTER WAS FLOODED IN JOSHUA TREE. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF WATER WAS AT THE INTERSECTION OF OAKEY AND DECATUR IN LAS VEGAS. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED.
BADWATER ROAD AT BADWATER BASIN WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
HIGHWAY 95 WAS CLOSED JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 40 DUE TO FLOODING.
RANCH ROAD WEST OF HIGHWAY 168 WAS CLOSED IN MOAPA DUE TO FLOODWATERS.

SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER WERE REPORTED BEHIND THE IMPERIAL PALACE PARKING GARAGE FLOWING ACROSS KOVAL LANE. A NUMBER OF OTHER ROADS WERE FLOODED THROUGHOUT THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY WEST AND EAST OF THE STRIP WITH SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER. THIS EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
A SPOTTER MEASURED A 101 MPH WIND GUST IN KINGMAN.
TWENTY POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF KINGMAN. STOPS SIGNS WERE BENT AT A 45 DEGREE ANGLE.
TWENTY POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF KINGMAN. STOPS SIGNS WERE BENT AT A 45 DEGREE ANGLE.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS TORE A CARPORT OFF A HOME AND BLEW IT DOWN THE STREET. THE CARPORT WAS THEN WRAPPED AROUND A UTILITY POLE.
OATMAN HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODWATERS FROM THURSDAY EVENING THROUGH FRIDAY MORNING. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
SEVERAL ROADS WERE CLOSED IN THE KINGMAN AREA DUE TO FLASH FLOODING...INCLUDING INTERSTATE 40 AT THE INTERSECTION OF U.S. HIGHWAY 93 AND BANK STREET.
DIAMOND BAR ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODWATERS. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
YUCCA MESA ROAD WAS REPORTED CLOSED BETWEEN BARRON AND SUNBURST ROADS.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED SEVERAL VEHICLES STUCK IN THE MUD NEAR NEEDLES HIGHWAY AND SHERMAN WAY.

US HIGHWAY 95 HAS BOULDERS ON IT BETWEEN SILVER CREEK AND RIVERVIEW IN BULLHEAD CITY. THE BULLHEAD CITY POLICE DEPARTMENT ALSO REPORTED MOST SIDE STREETS IN BULLHEAD CITY ALSO HAD WATER ON THEM. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
BETWEEN 6OO AND 800 AM...VISIBILITY NEAR ZERO DUE TO BLOWING DUST...SAND AND GRAVEL. PEAK WIND GUSTS DURING THIS PERIOD ESTIMATED AT 65 MPH.
VISIBILITY IN SILVER PEAK WAS REDUCED TO ABOUT 100 FEET DUE TO BLOWING DUST ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A LARGE PINE TREE WAS BLOWN OVER BY WINDS, LIKELY FROM A THUNDERSTORM, AND RESULTED IN THE TREE BLOCKING ROUTE 266 NEAR LIDA. 4 LIGHTNING STRIKES WERE NOTED IN THE AREA.
NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL CLOSED U.S. 95 FROM RAILROAD PASS TO SEARCHLIGHT BECAUSE OF NEAR ZERO VISIBILITIES DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
THREE AREAS WITH ROCKS AND OTHER APPROXIMATELY SOFTBALL SIZED DEBRIS ON THE ROAD UP TO THE PEAK.
ARIZONA DPS REPORTS THAT WATER BRIEFLY COVERED HIGHWAY 93 NEAR WIKIEUP

RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAIN RESULTED IN WATER FROM WASHES SPILLING 8 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER AND DEBRIS ONTO AHA MACAV PARKWAY NEAR THE AVI CASINO NEAR THE CLARK-SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY LINE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON CALLS TO CHP AND AVI CASINO STAFF.
HEAVY RAINFALL RESULTED IN WATER COVERING US 95 JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 40 IN NEEDLES. THE ROAD HAD TO BE CLOSED AS A RESULT.
PIPE SPRINGS NATIONAL MONUMENT REPORTED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST TO 64 MPH AT 251 PM.
PIPE SPRINGS NM REPORTS TSTM WIND GUST TO 64 MPH AT 251 PM.
A SPOTTER REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 60 MPH AT 706 PM IN LOGANDALE.
SPOTTER REPORTS WIND GUST TO 60 MPH AT 706 PM. PEA SIZED HAIL AND VERY HEAVY RAIN ALSO REPORTED.
A WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT SEVERAL CARS WERE DENTED BY LARGE HAIL ON INTERSTATE 15 BETWEEN MOAPA AND MESQUITE.
SPOTTER IN OVERTON REPORTS WATER OVER LINEMAN AND FRAINER AND PAVEMENT WASHED OUT ON GOUEBLER AND HEYER IN LOGANDALE. NAMED ROADS ARE IMPASSIBLE DUE TO DELAYED ONSET FLOODING.
FIRE STATION 43 IN MEADVIEW REPORTED WATER AT LEAST 6 INCHES DEEP FLOWING ON ROADS NEAR THE STATION. THEY ALSO REPORTED LATER THAT THE WATER WAS CLOSE TO TWO AND A HALF FEET DEEP AT ONE POINT.
BOTH A MOTEL EMPLOYEE AND FIRE STATION 43 ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUSTS IN MEADVIEW FROM A THUNDERSTORM. THIS EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
FIRE STATION 43 RELAYED A REPORT OF A ROOF TORN OFF OF A MOBILE HOME IN MEADVIEW.
FIRE STATION 43 RELAYED A REPORT OF A ROOF TORN OFF OF A MOBILE HOME IN MEADVIEW. SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE ALSO KNOCKED DOWN. A JOSHUA TREE ALSO FELL DOWN.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED THAT WATER TOOK OUT SEVERAL SMALL SIGNS AND SHRUBS IN MEADVIEW. ALSO A DRIVEWAY WAS UNDERCUT AND WASHED OUT BY FLOODWATERS.
A WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST BLEW A BIG RIG OFF OF INTERSTATE 15 JUST SOUTH OF OUTLET CENTER DRIVE NEAR BARSTOW.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED 55 TO 60 MPH WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM WHILE DRIVING ON INTERSTATE 15.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZE HAIL FELL ON INTERSTATE 15 NEAR BARSTOW FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
THE PARK SERVICE ESTIMATED AT LEAST TWO AND A HALF FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS PIERCE FERRY ROAD. THE FLASH FLOOD WAS OVER WITH BY 730 PM.
TRACTOR TRAILER OVERTURNED AROUND 1700 PDT BY HIGH WINDS AT MM 40 NEAR THE ELDORADO DRY LAKE BED ON US HIGHWAY 95.
THE PARK SERVICE ESTIMATED AT LEAST TWO AND A HALF FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS PIERCE FERRY ROAD. THE FLASH FLOOD WAS OVER WITH BY 730 PM.
TRACTOR TRAILER OVERTURNED AROUND 1700 PDT BY HIGH WINDS AT MM 40 NEAR THE ELDORADO DRY LAKE BED ON US HIGHWAY 95.
A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE TRAVELED TO WIKIEUP TO INSPECT THUNDERSTORM DAMAGE AND FOUND A SHED BLOWN OVER AND A ONE FOOT DIAMETER TREE DOWNED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES WERE KNOCKED DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN WIKIEUP. HIGHWAY 93 WAS CLOSED AS A RESULT.
ADOT WORKERS ESTIMATED 60 TO 70 MPH WIND GUSTS FROM A THUNDERSTORM IN WIKIEUP.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED CIMA ROAD WAS FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE FROM 310 PM THROUGH 330 PM.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED CIMA ROAD WAS FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE FROM 310 PM THROUGH 330 PM.
A SPOTTER REPORTED 8 TO 12 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ON HILTON AVENUE AT HIGWAY 62 IN YUCCA VALLEY WITH VEHICLES STUCK IN THE WATER.
A SPOTTER REPORTED LARGE ROCKS WERE WASHED ONTO HIGHWAY 62 AT WARREN VISTA.
THE PARK SERVICE SAID AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 190 ABOUT 4 MILES WEST OF DEATH VALLEY JUNCTION. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
SPOTTER MEASURED A TSTM WIND GUST TO 62 MPH AT 215 PDT IN KINGMAN.
SPOTTER REPORTS 10 TO 11 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN ROADWAYS IN GOLDEN VALLEY. ONE INCH OF RAIN FELL BETWEEN 130 AND 230 PM PDT.

CHP REPORTS 3 TO 4 FEET OF MUD WAS WASHED ONTO HIGHWAY 395 AT DUNMOVIN. THE HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED AS A RESULT WITH THE SOUTHBOUND LANES RE-OPENED AT 1025 PM ON AUGUST 26TH WITH BOTH NORTHBOUND LANES RE-OPENED AT 945 AM ON AUGUST 27TH.
CHP REPORTS THREE FEET OF MUD OVER US HIGHWAY 395 JUST SOUTH OF OLANCHA. ROADWAY REMAINS CLOSED WHILE DEBRIS IS REMOVED.
CW4367 REPORTED A 59 MPH TSTM WIND GUST AT 327 PDT.
NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTS WATER AND DEBRIS OVER BADWATER ROAD SOUTH OF BADWATER.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS ESTIMATED AT 45 TO 50 MPH DAMAGED A CARPORT ON A MOBILE HOME AT THE FRONTIER VILLAGE MOBILE HOME PARK.
BARSTOW PD REPORTS THAT WATER WAS FLOWING OVER ROADWAYS AND CARRYING DEBRIS.
CHP REPORTS DEBRIS FROM FLOODING ON STATE HIGHWAY 190 JUST WEST OF PANAMINT VALLEY ROAD.
SPOTTER REPORTS 1.25 INCHES OF RAIN SINCE 405 PM. ALL WASHES ARE FULL AND ROADWAYS ARE FLOODED.
A 60 MPH WIND GUST WAS ESTIMATED BY A CO-OP OBSERVER IN AMBOY IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.
THE BULLHEAD CITY FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED SEVERAL AWNINGS BLEW OFF MOBILE HOMES DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN BULLHEAD CITY BY WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED AT 60 KNOTS.
THE PARK SERVICE MEASURED A 70 MPH WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR ON LAKE MEAD BEFORE THE ANEMOMETER BROKE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL WAS REPORTED IN THE WAL-MART PARKING LOT IN BULLHEAD CITY.
WALLMART PARKING LOT IN BULLHEAD CITY
US HWY 95 10 MILES SOUTH OF LAUGHLIN CUT-OFF WASHED OUT. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
THE INTERSECTION OF LAKESIDE AND CLEARWATER WAS FLOODED OUT, AND OTHER INTERSECTIONS WILL LIKELY BE CLOSED SOON.
DEBRIS WAS REPORTED ON SILVER CREEK ROAD ON TWO EASTBOUND LANES RESULTING IN A ROAD CLOSURE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
WATER AT A LEAST A FOOT DEEP RESULTED IN THE SWIFTWATER RESCUE OF FIVE PEOPLE ON RIVERVIEW DRIVE.

THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED THUNDERSTORM WINDS CAUSED AT LEAST 300,000 DOLLARS IN DAMAGES TO FLOATING DOCKS AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR AND A NEARBY GOVERNMENT DOCK IN THE LAKE MEAD NATIONAL RECREATION AREA. A FUEL DOCK WAS ALSO PULLED APART AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR AND DAMAGED AND THE ABOVE DAMAGE TOTAL INCLUDES THIS. ADDITIONALLY AT LEAST 2 PRIVATE BOATS WERE DAMAGED AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
THE PARK SERVICE MEASURED A 70 MPH WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR ON LAKE MEAD BEFORE THE ANEMOMETER BROKE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
THE WIND GUST WAS MEASURED BY A DAVIS WIZARD II WEATHER STATION.
DIAMOND BAR ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING BEGINNING AT 1115 AM AND WAS STILL CLOSED AS OF 1210 PM.
1.40 INCHES OF RAIN THROUGH 1111 AM. AT 1150 AM A 25 FOOT WIDE SWATH OF WATER AND DEBRIS WAS OVER THE ROAD. THE ROAD WAS STILL CLOSED AT 1255 PM.
HIGHWAY 95 SOUTH WAS CLOSED AT RAILROAD PASS DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
PATTERSON GRADE ON PIERCE FERRY ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO DEBRIS. 1.30 INCHES OF RAIN FELL AT THE SPOTTERS LOCATION.
APPROXIMATELY ONE FOOT OF WATER WAS RUSHING THROUGH THE INTERSECTION OF GUBLER AND HEYER STREETS. THE ROAD BED WAS BEGINNING TO WASH AWAY.
HIGHWAY 93 WAS BLOCKED AT MILE MARKER 109 DUE TO DEBRIS WASHING ACROSS THE ROAD.
HIGHWAY 95 SOUTH WAS CLOSED AGAIN. WATER WAS RUSHING DOWN THE SIDE OF RIVER MOUNTAIN.
SMALL HAIL ALSO BEGAN AT 430 PM.
6-8 INCHES OF WATER OVER THE ROAD AT LOUISE AVE AND STOCKTON HILL RD IN KINGMAN AZ.

TILES WERE DAMAGED ON THE ROOF OF TWO HOMES IN THE RANCHO SANTA FE SUBDIVISION OF KINGMAN. NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS TWO BRIEF TOUCHDOWNS OF AN EF0 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 75 MPH. THE TOUCHDOWNS WERE LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 1.2 MILES APART.

PUBLIC REPORTS THAT A TORNADO TORE ROOF TILES OFF HIS HOUSE NEAR MOUNTAIN TRAIL AND PINTO IN KINGMAN. PD OFFICER REPORTED FUNNEL CLOUD AT 0515 PM MST IN THE AREA. POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO ANOTHER HOME NEAR OMAHA STREET IN KINGMAN. ONLY TWO HOMES KNOWN TO HAVE DAMAGE.
TILES WERE DAMAGED ON THE ROOF OF A HOUSE IN THE RANCHO SANTA FE SUBDIVISION OF KINGMAN. THIS WAS ORIGINALLY REPORTED AS A TORNADO. A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE WILL SURVEY THE DAMAGE TODAY.
A COCORAHS OBSERVER ESTIMATED A WIND GUST OF 60 MPH FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
A COCORAHS OBSERVER IN BARSTOW REPORTED HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE BROKE WINDOWS ON THEIR HOUSE. THE HAIL ALSO SHREDDED LEAVES ON TREES.
DEEP WATER FLOWING IN THE STREETS WITH LIMBS AND HAIL IN THE FLOODWATERS. FLOODING STARTED AT AROUND 440 PM AND WAS ONGOING AT 450 PM.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL WITH SMALLER HAIL ACCUMULATING IN THE STREETS. HAIL LASTED FROM 440 TO 450 PM.
SEVERAL HOMES AND APARTMENTS HAD ROOF DAMAGE FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS IN BARSTOW. TWO FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS WERE ALSO KNOCKED DOWN. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.

HAIL OF AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE BROKE ALL WINDOWS ON THE EAST SIDE OF A MOBILE HOME IN DOLAN SPRINGS.THE HAIL ALSO DENTED THE HOOD OF A TRUCK AND BUSTED A GUTTER ON THE MOBILE HOME. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED WINDS GUSTING TO 60 MPH IN BARSTOW FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
A GAS STATION EMPLOYEE REPORTED HAIL RANGING FROM A HALF INCH TO ONE INCH IN DIAMETER IN BARSTOW.
FLOWING WATER 1 FOOT DEEP AT OWENS AVE AND CHRISTY LN.
A CAR AND TRAILOR WERE FLOATED DOWN A WASH IN ECHO BAY BY FLASH FLOODWATERS.DEBRIS WAS ALSO WASHED ONTO THE NORTHSHORE ROAD BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 14 AND 16. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
HEAVY RAINS PRODUCED FLOWING WATER/DEBRIS DOWNWASH AND INTO THE CALLVILLE BAY AREA.
HEAVY RAINS PRODUCE FLASH FLOODING IN THE ECHO BAY AREA WITH A CAR/TRAILOR BEING REPORTED BEING WASHED OFF THE ROAD.
HEAVY RAIN WASHED A NUMBER OF BOULDERS ONTO WHITNEY PORTAL ROAD WEST OF LONE PINE. WORK CREWS HAD TO BE DISPATCHED TO CLEAR THEM OFF THE ROAD.
A SPOTTER REPORTED AT LEAST A FOOT OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 395 JUST SOUTH OF OLANCHA. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
A SMALL MUDSLIDE TOOK PLACE ON KYLE CANYON ROAD ON MT. CHARLESTON WITH DEBRIS ONTO ONE LANE OF THE ROAD. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED TWO MALES WERE REPORTED STUCK IN A VEHICLE DUE TO WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF IRWIN ROAD AND COOPER CITY ROAD.

TWO PEOPLE AND A DOG WERE RESCUED AT RED ROCK CANYON SUNDAY EVENING AFTER GETTING STUCK NEAR FLOODED WASHES. THE WATER WAS ABOUT 4 FEET DEEP AND 20 FEET WIDE AND MOVING ABOUT 10 MPH. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
VARIOUS COUNTY ROADS IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK WERE REPORTED TO HAVE DEBRIS ON THEM AS WELL AS FLOODWATERS OF VARYING DEPTHS SINCE AT LEAST 5 PM TODAY.
INTERSECTION OF CEDAR RD AND JASPER RD FLOODED WITH A VEHICLE POSSIBLY STALLED IN THE WATER.
A DISABLED VEHICLE WAS STUCK IN FLOODWATERS JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 395 ON KENNEDY MEADOW ROAD.
SEVERAL ROADS ON FORT IRWIN WERE WASHED OUT OVERNIGHT AND SOME WERE STILL FLOODED. FORT IRWIN ROAD SOUTH OF THE BASE WAS ALSO FLOODED. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
HIGHWAY 159 WAS CLOSED AT RED ROCK CANYON AND THE RED ROCK CANYON SCENIC DRIVE WAS ALSO CLOSED DUE TO ROCKS AND OTHER DEBRIS ON THE ROAD.
YUCCA MESA ROAD AT YUCCA WASH AND INDIO AVENUE AT YUCCA CREEK WERE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. SOFTBALL SIZED ROCKS WERE ON YUCCA TRAIL WITH WATER FLOWING A FOOT DEEP ON THIS ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED JASPER ROAD AT CEDAR ROAD WAS FLOODED WITH 8 TO 10 INCHES OF WATER.
LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED THAT THE CLARK COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT HAS CLOSED ARROYO ROAD NEAR THE TOWN OF BLUE DIAMOND.
LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED THAT THE CLARK COUNTY PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT HAD CLOSED BONNIE SPRINGS ROAD NEAR THE TOWN OF BONNIE SPRINGS.
NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED MUD ON STATE ROUTE 160 NEAR MILE MARKER 22. THE ROAD WAS NOT CLOSED BUT WAS CLEARED UP AS OF 1130 PM.
FLOODING ON EASTSIDE...VOLUNTEERS NEEDED FOR SANDBAGGING AND SANDBAGS...EAST MESQUITE BLVD AND COTTON WOOD RD 18 INCHES OF WATER FROM THE VIRGIN RIVER.
HEAVY RAIN AND RUNOFF RESULTED IN MUD AND ROCKS COVERING PORTIONS OF GRAND TETON DRIVE FROM BUFFALO DRIVE TO DECATUR BLVD. PORTIONS OF FARM ROAD ALSO WERE COVERED WITH SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER.
NATIONAL TRAILS HWY AND HODGE ROAD COVERED IN DIRT AND MUD.
FLOODING ALONG WARM SPRINGS ROAD IN THREE LOCATIONS...WITH 2 TO 3 FEET ACROSS THE ROAD WHERE PAHRANAGAT WASH CROSSES.

HEAVY STREET FLOODING THROUGHOUT PAHRUMP. RUNOFF OFF OF THE ALLUVIAL FAN ACROSS WHEELER PASS...ACROSS HWY 160 AT CALVADA WITH A DEPTH OF 12 TO 18 INCHES. 20 TO 25 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS GAMEBIRD ROAD. WATER FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 372.
AN SUV WAS STUCK IN RUSHING FLOODWATERS ON MOJAVE RANCH ROAD IN JOSHUA TREE. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.
SPOTTER REPORTED HWY160 FROM LAS VEGAS TO PAHRUMP SAYS WATER ON THE ROAD...MUD AND DEBRIS COVER THE ROAD.
PORTIONS OF SEVERAL ROADS WERE CLOSED IN YUCCA VALLEY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.

A CAR WAS RESCUED FROM FLOODWATERS AFTER BEING STUCK ON OLD WOMAN SPRINGS ROAD AT PIPES CANYON ROAD. RECHE ROAD IN LANDERS WAS ALSO FILLED WITH DEBRIS AND HAD TO BE CLEARED BY A GRADER AND SKIP LOADER.
WATER RUNNING OVER HWY 178...FIRE DEPARTMENT WENT TO RECUSE 7 PEOPLE TRAPPED IN VECHILES.
ROADS IN THE TOWN OF TECOPA HAVE FLOODED...4WD NEEDED ASSISTANCE TO GET OUT OF TRAPPED WATER.
SPOTTER REPORTED WATER IS 6 INCHES OVER THE ROAD SURFACE NEAR LENWOOD ROAD...HE STATED IT WAS KIND OF HARD TO PASS AND WAS STARTING TO SEE SPLASH OVER.
SPRING VALLEY STATE PARK AND ECHO CANYON STATE PARK WERE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
LARGE ROCKS AND MUD SLID OFF A CLIFF AND COVERED HIGHWAY 93 NEAR THE HACIENDA CASINO. SOME WATER WAS ALSO ON THE ROAD. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.

NEARLY EVERY MAJOR WASH IN VALLEY OF FIRE STATE PARK RAN OVER ROADS WITHIN THE PARK AS WELL AS ON HIGHWAY 169 ABOUT 6 MILES WEST OF THE PARK COVERING THEM WITH VARYING AMOUNTS OF ROCK AND MUD. PARK OFFICIALS CLOSED THE PARK TUESDAY INTO WEDNESDAY MORNING AND HAD TO SEND EQUIPMENT TO CLEAR AND GRADE ROADS. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
NORTH SHORE DRIVE IN THE LAKE MEAD NATIONAL RECREATION AREA WAS IMPASSABLE NEAR MILE MARKER 44 DUE TO FLOODING.
TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER ESTIMATED A WIND GUST FROM A THUNDERSTORM OF 60 MPH. VISIBILITY WAS REDUCED TO 100 FEET IN RAIN AND DEBRIS.
SEVERAL ROADS WERE FLOODED AND CLOSED IN BULLHEAD CITY. WATER WAS UP TO A FOOT DEEP ON RIVERVIEW DRIVE. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED.
A LEVEE ALONG THE MOJAVE RIVER BROKE AND FLOODED AREAS ALONG SOAPMINE ROAD IN BARSTOW. MUD COVERED A FEW STREETS IN THE AREA. PEOPLE AND HORSES WERE EVACUATED.
